steps.title: Identify the key structural contrast
steps.content: The notes clearly distinguish the two materials: graphite has stacked graphene sheets, while carbon nanotubes have sheets wrapped into a cylindrical shape. To emphasize this difference as requested, the answer must state both structural forms directly.

steps.title: Match the structural descriptions to the answer
steps.content:
Choice D directly states both structural forms with emphasis: “The graphene sheets in graphite are stacked, whereas in carbon nanotubes, they form a cylinder.” The word whereas marks the contrast, and both details come from the notes.

Choices A, B, and C fail to effectively emphasize the specific difference between graphite and nanotubes.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
